data.title: Banc of California signals continued margin expansion and robust buybacks with CET1 above 10%
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Banc of California (BANC) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Jared Wolff highlighted "double-digit earnings per share growth and continued momentum across all of our key performance drivers," stating that Q3 EPS rose 23% quarter-over-quarter to $0.38, driven by "positive operating leverage and continuing to expand our net interest margin." Wolff emphasized the repurchase of 2.2 million shares in Q3, totaling 13.6 million since the program began, and noted "Repurchases have totaled $185 million, more than half of our $300 million repurchase authorization."
  • Wolff pointed to core deposit growth, with noninterest-bearing deposits up 9% and now at 28% of total deposits, reduction in broker deposits, and a lowered total cost of deposits to 2.08%. He detailed "loan production and disbursements remained healthy at $2.1 billion" and a focus on exiting criticized loans while maintaining a stable core loan portfolio.
  • Wolff reported "credit quality remained stable with criticized loans down 4% quarter-over-quarter and special mention loans down 24%." He described classified loan balance increases as "a timing issue related to a $50 million CRE loan" and risk rating framework updates in the Venture Banking portfolio, clarifying, "all of those loans are performing and on accrual status with no delinquencies greater than 30 days."
  • CFO Joseph Kauder stated, "For the third quarter, we reported net income of $59.7 million or $0.38 per diluted share, which was up 23% from the adjusted EPS of $0.31 in the prior quarter." Kauder noted net interest income rose 5% to $253 million, with net interest margin expanding to 3.22%, and commented, "Average yield on loans increased 12 basis points to 6.05%." He added, "Noninterest expenses of $185.7 million were relatively flat across most expense categories as we continue to maintain disciplined expense controls while supporting our growth initiatives."

Outlook

  • Kauder stated, "We remain on track with our 2025 guidance. We continue to expect loan growth for the full year to be in the mid-single-digit range and net interest margin to remain within our 3.20% to 3.30% target range for the fourth quarter."
  • Wolff projected, "I expect if we're 3.20% to 3.30% right now, we're going to end up -- obviously, we're going to end up there given that we are at 3.18% in the fourth quarter...I would expect the jumping off point for '26 is going to be 3.25% to 3.35% or whatever it is, that gives us some flexibility."

Financial Results

  • The company reported net income of $59.7 million and net interest income of $253 million. Net interest margin reached 3.22% and average yield on loans was 6.05%. Noninterest income was $34.3 million, up 5% from last quarter. Expenses were $185.7 million, with an adjusted efficiency ratio improving to 58%.
  • CET1 capital ratio increased to 10.14%, and tangible book value per share rose 3% to $16.99.

Q&A

  • Jared David Shaw, Barclays: Asked about credit trends and NDFI lending exposure, referencing recent industry issues. Wolff clarified, "We were mentioned because we had a loan to a related borrower. But our loan to that borrower was not an NDFI loan. It was a pure real estate loan...we think we're well secured, and we think there's plenty of collateral there."
  • Shaw followed up on margin guidance. Wolff stated, "We are liability sensitive when you factor in the ECRs. And so we do expect our margin to expand...our margin expansion is really being driven by this loan production that we're seeing."
  • Timur Braziler, Wells Fargo: Inquired about margin sensitivity to rate cuts. Wolff replied, "They're definitely not punitive to us. We are, at worst case, neutral with rate cuts when you take out ECR."
  • Matthew Clark, Piper Sandler: Asked about mid-single-digit growth guidance. Wolff responded, "It would suggest that we'd have to have some outsized growth this quarter, and we'll see if we hit it...on a core basis on the loan, when you strip out the loans that were sold...we'll probably hit the 4% to 6% range."
  • Anthony Elian, JPMorgan: Pressed on buyback aggressiveness. Wolff answered, "We will be opportunistic without saying exactly when we're going to do it."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts repeatedly raised concerns about credit exposure, deposit growth sustainability, and the pace of buybacks, often pressing for clarification on risk and capital deployment.
  • Management maintained a confident and measured tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, offering detailed explanations and using phrases such as "we believe Banc of California is well positioned" and "I feel very comfortable with what we do."
  • Compared to last quarter, analyst sentiment remained skeptical but focused, while management confidence increased, particularly regarding balance sheet strength and future guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for loan growth and margin remained consistent with Q2, but with more explicit statements about achieving low 3.20s NIM in Q4 and a projected starting point for 2026 of 3.25% to 3.35%.
  • The capital return program accelerated, with buybacks totaling $185 million and CET1 exceeding 10% versus a more conservative approach noted in Q2.
  • Core deposit trends improved, noninterest-bearing deposits increased, and broker deposits were further reduced from Q2.
  • Expense control remained tight, with noninterest expenses flat, contrasting with Q2's anticipation of higher expenses.
  • Management tone shifted from cautious optimism to greater confidence in sustained earnings growth and balance sheet quality.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management addressed industry NDFI lending concerns, clarifying their portfolio's structure and emphasizing robust collateral and anti-fraud measures.
  • Cautious approach remains in office lending and government-tenant exposures, with deliberate exits from these sectors.
  • Classification changes in Venture Banking were procedural, not indicative of underlying credit weakness.
  • Analysts continued to question the timing and scale of buybacks, deposit growth challenges, and the sustainability of current credit quality.

Final Takeaway

Banc of California management emphasized strong sequential earnings growth, robust capital and liquidity positions, and disciplined capital return through share repurchases. The company reaffirmed its guidance for mid-single-digit loan growth and net interest margin in the 3.20% to 3.30% range, with a projected margin expansion into 2026. Management underscored their confidence in the bank's business model, proactive credit risk management, and the strategic flexibility to continue opportunistic buybacks while maintaining CET1 above 10%.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Welltower may be near £4B deal to buy Barchester Healthcare - report
data.description:
• Welltower (NYSE:WELL) could announce a ~£4B deal to buy Barchester Healthcare as early as tomorrow, according to a  posting on X from a Sky News reporter. 
  • Barchester Health is one of Britain's biggest nursing home operators. Welltower and Banchester didn't respond to the Sky News reporter's request for comment.
  • Welltower is set to report Q3 results on Monday.
